topic: Absolute Value Inequalities and Their Applications

questions.question: Solve the inequality $|x - 5| < 2$. What is the solution set?
A recipe for chicken parmesan requires $|2x - 5|$ ounces of breadcrumbs. If $x$ must be between 2 and 4, what is the range of possible breadcrumb amounts?
The height of a building is $|3x + 2|$ feet. If the height must be between 10 and 20 feet, what is the solution set for $x$?
A carpenter needs to cut a board to $|x - 10|$ inches. If the board must be between 5 and 15 inches, what is the solution set for $x$?
Graph the solution set of $|x + 2| leq 4$ on a number line.
The cost of shipping a package is $|2x - 10|$ dollars. If the cost must be between 5 and 15 dollars, what is the range of possible package weights?
A chef needs to mix $|x + 3|$ cups of flour. If the recipe requires between 2 and 6 cups of flour, what is the solution set for $x$?
Solve the inequality $|3x - 2| > 7$. What is the solution set?
Model the situation: A bridge has a height restriction of $|2x - 5|$ feet. If trucks can be at most 12 feet tall, what is the range of possible values for $x$?

A baker is making a cake that requires $|x - 3|$ cups of sugar. If the recipe can use at most 5 cups of sugar, what is the solution set for $x$? Graph the solution set on a number line and express it in interval notation.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand the concept of absolute value inequalities.
Tip 2: Provide examples of real-world applications to help students model situations using absolute value inequalities.
Tip 3: Encourage students to graph solution sets on a number line and express them in interval notation.
